title: USMNT projected World Cup lineup vs Bosnia and Herzegovina
original_text:
June 29, 2026, 6:20 p.m. ET
SANTA CLARA, CA — The U.S. men's national team is likely to stick with what's been working in its World Cup round of 32 match against Bosnia and Herzegovina on Wednesday, July 1.
The USMNT hammered Paraguay 4-1 to begin the World Cup, then rolled out a similar lineup (minus the injured Christian Pulisic) and beat Australia 2-0 in its second game.
With the group safely clinched, Mauricio Pochettino made wholesale changes against Turkey, starting a team almost entirely comprised of back-ups in a 3-2 defeat.
With everything on the line at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, Pochettino's lineup shouldn't have many surprises.
Here is who the USA is likely to start against Bosnia and Herzegovina:
Shop USMNT World Cup tickets
Goalkeeper: Matt Freese
Freese sat against Turkey, giving Matt Turner the chance to play in his second World Cup. The team's starting goalkeeper will be back on Wednesday.
Defenders: Sergiño Dest, Alex Freeman, Tim Ream, Chris Richards, Antonee Robinson
With all five of the team's starting defenders benched against Turkey, the U.S. turned in a shaky display in the back. Luckily, all five are rested and healthy for the knockouts.
Midfielders: Malik Tillman, Tyler Adams, Weston McKennie
The team's new "MMA" midfield should be back starting against Bosnia and Herzegovina. McKennie is the one U.S. player who started all three group games.
Forwards: Christian Pulisic, Folarin Balogun
Pulisic returned against Turkey and looked no worse for wear after missing out against Australia with a calf injury. He should be back in the lineup alongside Balogun.
USMNT predicted XI vs Bosnia and Herzegovina: Freese; A. Robinson, Ream, Richards, Freeman, Dest; McKennie, Adams, Tillman; Pulisic, Balogun
USA 2026 World Cup schedule
The USA national team begins the World Cup in Group D and ranked No. 16 in the FIFA World Rankings.
June 12: USA 4-1 Paraguay. SoFi Stadium, Inglewood, California
June 19: USA 2-0 Australia. Lumen Field, Seattle
June 25: USA 2-3 Turkey. Sofi Stadium, Inglewood, California
July 1: USA vs. Bosnia and Herzegovina, 8 p.m. ET, Levi's Stadium, Santa Clara, California
Streaming available on Fubo or the Fox One App. Spanish language broadcasts are available on Telemundo and Peacock.
Watch every World Cup game with Fubo
USA World Cup roster
Goalkeepers: Chris Brady (Chicago Fire), Matt Freese (New York City FC), Matt Turner (New England Revolution)
Defenders: Max Arfsten (Columbus Crew), Sergiño Dest (PSV), Alex Freeman (Villarreal), Mark McKenzie (Toulouse), Tim Ream (Charlotte FC), Chris Richards (Crystal Palace), Antonee Robinson (Fulham), Miles Robinson (FC Cincinnati), Joe Scally (Borussia Mönchengladbach), Auston Trusty (Celtic)
Midfielders: Tyler Adams (AFC Bournemouth), Sebastian Berhalter (Vancouver Whitecaps), Weston McKennie (Juventus), Gio Reyna (Borussia Mönchengladbach), Cristian Roldan (Seattle Sounders), Malik Tillman (Bayer Leverkusen)
Forwards: Brenden Aaronson (Leeds United), Folarin Balogun (Monaco), Ricardo Pepi (PSV), Christian Pulisic (AC Milan), Tim Weah (Olympique Marseille), Haji Wright (Coventry City), Alejandro Zendejas (Club América)
Full rosters for 2026 World Cup teams
USA World Cup history
The U.S. national team is back in the World Cup for the 12th time after last qualifying in 2022. The country's best result in the men's tournament is third place (1930).
How did USA qualify for the World Cup?
USA, ranked No. 16 in the FIFA Men's World Rankings, earned a berth in the 2026 World Cup as hosts.
Where is the USA national team base camp?
Great Park - Irvine, California
Shop USMNT World Cup tickets
The 2026 World Cup is the largest ever, and USA TODAY is going all in with reporters on the ground in all 16 host cities across three countries to cover 48 teams vying for the trophy.
